title: Migrate from ESLint + Prettier to Biome
description: Replace the ESLint (next/core-web-vitals) + Prettier toolchain with Biome (@biomejs/biome) for unified, faster lint+format. Add biome.json (import existing rules via 'biome migrate eslint --write' and 'biome migrate prettier --write'), replace the lint/prettier npm scripts with biome, and remove eslint*/prettier deps and configs. Known trade-off: Biome doesn't replicate Next-specific lint rules (e.g. next/no-img-element) — review whether any are worth keeping. Verify lint+format pass on the codebase.
close_reason: Migrated to Biome 2.5. Added biome.json (Prettier-matched: 2-space, double quotes, semicolons), replaced lint/prettier scripts with biome check / biome check --write, removed eslint-config-next + prettier + their configs. Disabled noFallthroughSwitchClause (intentional per CLAUDE.md), noExplicitAny + noNonNullAssertion (pervasive intentional). Fixed real findings: type=button on 10 buttons, Map->MapIcon alias, removed unused lodash reverse, node: protocol/radix/Number.isNaN autofixes. Excluded *.css (Biome can't parse Tailwind v4 @theme). Verified: biome check exit 0 (4 advisory warnings), 106 tests pass, production build succeeds.

title: Replace CSV parsing with DAILYSCORESVIRTUAL JSON endpoint
description: Switch the flight data source from the getlogfilescsv CSV to the BGA JSON endpoint /API/DAILYSCORESVIRTUAL (CORS-enabled, see trophies-xeh notes). Work: build a typed fetch with the required query params (Season, Club=CAM, the FlightDate Desc, TPoints sidx quirk, skip/take pagination using totalCount), replace parseCsv + flightCsvSpec SPEC with a JSON->Flight field mapping, and drop papaparse. Net: cleaner typed data, no CSV parsing. NOTE: DAILYSCORESVIRTUAL has ScoringDistance/XCPoints/turnpoints/ladders but NOT TaskDistance/HandicapDistance — task-distance handling is covered by the follow-on enrichment issue.

title: Remove /api/flights backend; fetch BGA CSV directly client-side
description: The /api/flights Next API route is a thin proxy: CORS workaround, multi-year fan-out (start..end), CSV->JSON parse (papaparse), and Cache-Control headers. There is NO SSR anywhere in the app, so this route is the only server-side feature. Verified the BGA CSV endpoint already supports cross-origin browser calls (it reflects the Origin header and sets access-control-allow-credentials), so the proxy is unnecessary. Plan: move the fetch into useFlights (Promise.all over the 3 years), run parseCsv/papaparse client-side, delete src/pages/api/flights.ts (and csv.ts/flightCsvSpec.ts move to src/lib), drop axios in favour of fetch, update tests. Result: app becomes a pure static SPA hostable on GitHub Pages/Cloudflare. Trade-off: lose shared server-side caching (each visitor hits BGA directly, ~3 requests) — negligible for club traffic; URL/club code already public (no auth).
notes:
CORS evidence (2026-06-22): GET api.bgaladder.net/api/getlogfilescsv/2024/CGC with Origin header returns access-control-allow-origin reflecting the origin + access-control-allow-credentials: true.
API investigation (2026-06-22, via playwright). The BGA SPA uses a JSON API (all endpoints CORS-enabled — reflect Origin). Club code is CAM (not CGC). Relevant endpoints:
1. /API/DAILYSCORESVIRTUAL?Season={y}&Month=0&Day=0&Club={code}&Region=-1&Launchpoint=0&glider=0&pilot=0&turnpoint=&Noengines=false&sidx=FlightDate%20Desc,%20TPoints&sord=desc&skip=0&take=200 -> {data:[...], totalCount}. Bulk flat flight list, paginated, club-filterable. This is the JSON replacement for getlogfilescsv. QUIRK: sidx MUST include ', TPoints' or data returns null. Has ScoringDistance, XCPoints, Speed, HandicapSpeed, TP1-4/Start/Finish, ladder booleans, Site, Glider, Registration. LACKS TaskDistance, HandicapDistance, and the 'X% Completed' string.
2. /API/FLIGHT/{flightId} -> full per-flight detail (74 fields). Nested Task object has TaskDistance (declared task distance, the value flightCsvSpec.ts:142-150 currently reverse-engineers from the % string), plus TP_OK, Barrels (sector radii), ShapeFactor, SectorType. Also top-level SecondPilot and rich Glider (Seats/Turbo/Vintage). One request per flight.
3. /API/LADDERS/{year}/{ladder}/{club}/{ALL}/-1/false/false/false/false/false -> per-pilot aggregate (top-6 flights with FlightIDs).
CAM 2024 = 396 flights. TRADE-OFF: DAILYSCORESVIRTUAL alone is a clean CSV->JSON swap but drops the taskDistance source; getting exact TaskDistance/TP_OK/SecondPilot needs FLIGHT/{id} (~396 req/season) -> consider enriching lazily or caching.

title: Set up repo metadata and CI
description: Public repo housekeeping for liwp/cgc-trophies: add a GitHub repo description and topics (nextjs, gliding, typescript, etc.), and add a CI workflow (.github/workflows) that runs lint, build, and test on push/PR.

title: Polish README for public audience
description: Now that cgc-trophies is a public GitHub repo, make the README read well for newcomers: what the app does (CGC annual trophies — fetches BGA Ladder data, scores flights against club trophy rules, shows winners), local setup steps (install, run dev), and ideally a screenshot of the winners page.

title: Evaluate JSON-only fields (SecondPilot, Seats/Turbo/Vintage) for trophy accuracy
description: The JSON flight detail exposes fields the CSV lacks: top-level SecondPilot, and Glider.Seats/Turbo/Vintage. Evaluate whether these improve trophy correctness — e.g. SecondPilot for genuine two-crew detection (vs the Complicity Cup's current registration-grouping + minPilots>=2 heuristic), and Seats/Turbo/Vintage for fleet/class-based trophies. Deliverable: decide which trophies benefit and update trophies.config.ts/eval.ts accordingly. Blocked by trophies-ouv.

title: Drop task-distance %-parsing hack via /API/FLIGHT/{id} enrichment
description: flightCsvSpec.ts:142-150 reverse-engineers the declared task distance by parsing 'X% Completed' out of the Task Achievement text and dividing scoringDistance by it. The JSON /API/FLIGHT/{id} endpoint exposes Task.TaskDistance directly (plus TP_OK, Barrels, ShapeFactor, SectorType, SecondPilot, and richer Glider: Seats/Turbo/Vintage). Enrich each flight via FLIGHT/{id} and remove the percentage hack. Cost: ~1 request per flight (CAM ~396/season) — do it lazily/cached or only for flights that feed trophies. Blocked by trophies-grb (JSON data source).

title: Evaluate migrating Next.js -> Vite + React (static SPA)
description: Once /api/flights is removed (trophies-xeh) the app has no server-side code at all, so Next.js is only acting as a static bundler. Evaluate moving to Vite + React (+ a lightweight router like React Router/wouter). The bulk of the code (eval.ts, csv parsing, stats.ts, trophies.config.ts, types) is framework-agnostic plain TS and ports as-is; only the handful of Pages-Router pages are Next-specific. Cheaper alternative to compare against: keep Next with output:'export' for a static build (near-zero work). Decision should fold in the bun migration (trophies-sz2), since Vite + bun is a natural combo. Deliverable: a recommendation (Vite vs Next static export) with rough effort estimate.

title: Migrate from npm to bun
description: Migrate the toolchain from npm to bun: replace package-lock.json with bun.lockb (bun install), update CLAUDE.md commands and any CI to use bun, and verify the Next.js 16 + Jest setup works under bun (dev server, production build, and the test suite all pass).
